messages.content: I have a word document that I open with excel. The word document has a table at the top. When the word document opens, I want to to capture the values of Cell B1 from the active excel sheet. Can word do this
No, Word alone cannot capture values from an Excel sheet. You would need to use VBA code in Word to interact with the Excel file and retrieve the value of Cell B1.
how do I do this

Here is an example VBA code you can use in Word to retrieve the value of Cell B1 from an active Excel sheet:

```
Sub GetCellValue()
    'Open Excel Application and workbook
    Set xlApp = CreateObject("Excel.Application")
    Set xlWB = xlApp.Workbooks.Open("C:\YourFilePath\YourWorkbookName.xlsx")
    
    'Get the value of Cell B1 from the active sheet
    cellValue = xlApp.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    
    'Close Excel Workbook and quit Excel Application
    xlWB.Close savechanges:=False
    xlApp.Quit
    
    'Display the value of Cell B1 in a message box
    MsgBox "The value of Cell B1 is: " & cellValue
End Sub
```

Make sure to replace "C:\YourFilePath\YourWorkbookName.xlsx" with the file path and name of your Excel workbook. 

You can then call this sub in your Word document to retrieve the value of Cell B1 from the Excel workbook. This code assumes you have the necessary permissions to access the Excel workbook from Word.

The excel document will always be open. In the excel workbook, there is a vba that calls to open the specific Word document. With the excel workbook open, after the word document has been called to open, I want the word document to capture the value of cell B1 from the active excel sheet

In that case, you can use the following VBA code in the Word document to get the value of Cell B1 from the active Excel sheet that called the Word document:

```
Sub GetCellValue()
    'Get the value of Cell B1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
    cellValue = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    
    'Display the value of Cell B1 in a message box
    MsgBox "The value of Cell B1 is: " & cellValue
End Sub
```

You can then call this sub from an event in the Word document (e.g., a button click) or from an event in the Excel VBA code that opened the Word document. 

Note that this code assumes that the active workbook in Excel is the one you want to retrieve the value of Cell B1 from, and that the active sheet in that workbook is the one you want to retrieve the value from. If you need to retrieve the value from a different workbook or sheet, you can modify the code accordingly.
rather than display the cell value of Cell Bi in a message box, can word append the value to the table within it

Yes, you can modify the code to append the value of Cell B1 to the table in the Word document. Here's an example code that appends the value to the first row of the table:

```
Sub AppendCellValueToTable()
    'Get the value of Cell B1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
    cellValue = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    
    'Get the Word table and append the value of Cell B1 to the first row
    With ActiveDocument.Tables(1)
        .Rows.Add
        .Cell(.Rows.Count, 1).Range.Text = cellValue
    End With
End Sub
```

This code assumes that the table you want to append the value to is the first table in the Word document. If you have multiple tables in the document, you can modify the code to target a different table by changing the `Tables(1)` reference.

You can call this sub from an event in the Word document or from an event in the Excel VBA code that opened the Word document.
What if my table in the word document has two rows and two columns

If your table in the Word document has two rows and two columns, you can modify the code to append the value of Cell B1 to the second cell in the first row. Here's an example code:

```
Sub AppendCellValueToTable()
    'Get the value of Cell B1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
    cellValue = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    
    'Get the Word table and append the value of Cell B1 to the second cell in the first row
    With ActiveDocument.Tables(1)
        .Rows.Add
        .Cell(.Rows.Count, 2).Range.Text = cellValue
    End With
End Sub
```

This code appends a new row to the table and then sets the value of the second cell in the first row to the value of Cell B1.

Again, you can call this sub from an event in the Word document or from an event in the Excel VBA code that opened the Word document.

Assuming that at the top of the word document there is a table with only two rows and two columns, then in the first row first column I want the values of B1 from the excel sheet. In the first row and second column I want the values of D1 from the excel sheet. In the Second row and the first column I want the values of A1 and in the second row and second column the values of C1

Sure, you can modify the code to append the values of cells A1, B1, C1, and D1 to the corresponding cells in the Word table. Here's an example code:

```
Sub AppendCellValuesToTable()
    'Get the values of cells A1, B1, C1, and D1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
    cellA1 = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("A1").Value
    cellB1 = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    cellC1 = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("C1").Value
    cellD1 = Application.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("D1").Value
    
    'Get the Word table and append the values to the corresponding cells
    With ActiveDocument.Tables(1)
        'First row
        .Cell(1, 1).Range.Text = cellB1
        .Cell(1, 2).Range.Text = cellD1
        
        'Second row
        .Cell(2, 1).Range.Text = cellA1
        .Cell(2, 2).Range.Text = cellC1
    End With
End Sub
```

This code gets the values of cells A1, B1, C1, and D1 from the active Excel sheet and then appends them to the corresponding cells in the Word table. 

Again, you can call this sub from an event in the Word document or from an event in the Excel VBA code that opened the Word document.
Now that we have the code, how do I insert it into the word document and how can I be sure of the name of the table in the word document.

To insert the code into the Word document, you need to open the VBA editor by pressing ALT + F11 and then create a new module where you will paste the code. 

Here are the steps to follow:
1. Open the Word document that you want to add the code to.
2. Hold down the ALT key and press F11. This will open the VBA editor.
3. In the VBA editor, right-click on the project name (usually the name of your document) in the Project Explorer window and select Insert > Module.
4. In the Module window that appears, copy and paste the code into the editor.
5. Save the module by selecting File > Save or pressing CTRL + S.

To ensure the name of the table in the Word document, you can use the following steps:

1. Click on the table to select it.
2. In the Ribbon, go to the Table Tools > Layout tab.
3. In the Properties group, you will see a box labeled "Table Name". This will have the name of the table. You can also edit the name from here if you need to.

Alternatively, you can use VBA to get the name of the table. Here's an example code that displays the name of the first table in the document in a message box:

```
Sub GetTableName()
    'Display the name of the first table in the document
    MsgBox ActiveDocument.Tables(1).Title
End Sub
```

You can call this sub from the Word document to get the name of the table. Note that the name of the table is the same as the title of the table, unless you have explicitly changed it using the "Table Name" property in the Layout tab.
Is there anything else you need assistance with?
with both the active workbook and the word document open, the vba code in the word document does not recognise that the excel workbook is open

If the Excel workbook is already open, you can try using the following code to get a reference to the Excel Application and Workbook objects:

```
Sub GetCellValue()
    'Get a reference to the Excel Application object
    Set xlApp = GetObject(, "Excel.Application")
    
    'Get a reference to the active Workbook object
    Set xlWB = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook
    
    'Get the value of Cell B1 from the active sheet
    cellValue = xlApp.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    
    'Close Excel Workbook and quit Excel Application
    xlWB.Close savechanges:=False
    xlApp.Quit
    
    'Display the value of Cell B1 in a message box
    MsgBox "The value of Cell B1 is: " & cellValue
End Sub
```

This code uses the `GetObject()` function to get a reference to the Excel Application object that is already open. 

Note that this code assumes that there is only one instance of Excel running. If there are multiple instances of Excel running, you may need to modify the code to get a reference to the correct instance of Excel.

This is what I have. Can you correct it. Sub AppendCellValuesToTable()

'Get a reference to the Excel Application object
Set xlApp = GetObject(, "Excel.Application")

'Get a reference to the active Workbook object
Set xlWB = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook

'Get the values of cells A1, B1, C1, and D1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
Set cellA1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("A1").Value
Set cellB1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
Set cellC1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("C1").Value
Set cellD1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("D1").Value

'Get the Word table and append the values to the corresponding cells
With ActiveDocument.Tables(1)
'First row
.Cell(1, 1).Range.Text = cellB1
.Cell(1, 2).Range.Text = cellD1

'Second row
.Cell(2, 1).Range.Text = cellA1
.Cell(2, 2).Range.Text = cellC1
End With
End Sub

Here's how you could correct the code:

```
Sub AppendCellValuesToTable()
    'Get a reference to the Excel Application object
    Set xlApp = GetObject(, "Excel.Application")
    
    'Get a reference to the active Workbook object
    Set xlWB = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook
    
    'Get the values of cells A1, B1, C1, and D1 from the active Excel sheet using the Excel object model
    cellA1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("A1").Value
    cellB1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("B1").Value
    cellC1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("C1").Value
    cellD1 = xlApp.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet.Range("D1").Value
    
    'Get the Word table and append the values to the corresponding cells
    With ActiveDocument.Tables(1)
        'First row
        .Cell(1, 1).Range.Text = cellB1
        .Cell(1, 2).Range.Text = cellD1
        
        'Second row
        .Cell(2, 1).Range.Text = cellA1
        .Cell(2, 2).Range.Text = cellC1
    End With
  
    'Close Excel Workbook and quit Excel Application
    xlWB.Close savechanges:=False
    xlApp.Quit
    
End Sub
```

This code should append the values of A1, B1, C1, and D1 to the corresponding cells in the table. 

I have also closed the Excel Workbook and quit the Excel Application at the end of the code. Note that if you only want to close the Workbook and not quit the Excel application (in case you have other open Workbook), you can use `xlWB.Close`.
great it worked. How do I now get word to run this code when the document is opened

To run the `AppendCellValuesToTable` code when the Word document is opened, you can use the `Document_Open` event in Word VBA.

Here's an example of how to do this:

1. Open the Word document that you want to add the code to.
2. Hold down the ALT key and press F11. This will open the VBA editor.
3. In the Project Explorer pane, find and select the project name (usually the document name).
4. From the Insert menu, choose New Module.
5. In the Module window, paste in the `AppendCellValuesToTable` code.
6. In the Project Explorer pane, double-click on "ThisDocument" to open its code module.
7. In the code module, enter the following code:

```vba
Private Sub Document_Open()
    AppendCellValuesToTable
End Sub
```

This code uses the `Document_Open` event, which is triggered when the Word document is opened. It simply calls the `AppendCellValuesToTable` subroutine that you created earlier.

Once you have added this code, save the document and close the VBA editor. Now, every time you open this document, it will automatically run the `AppendCellValuesToTable` subroutine and append the values of A1, B1, C1, and D1 to the corresponding cells in the table.
